---
title: "John Baldessari — Exhibition Catalogue: ISBN 3-88375-396-3"
subtitle: "Sprengel Museum Hannover"
description: "Catalogue of works by John Baldessari shown at the Sprengel Museum Hannover, sourced from OCR-processed exhibition catalogue (scan-2)."
date: today
execute:
  echo: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: load-xml
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

XML = Path("catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.xml")
tree = ET.parse(XML)
root = tree.getroot()

def t(el, tag):
    child = el.find(tag)
    return child.text.strip() if child is not None and child.text else ""

meta = root.find("meta")
exhibition_title = t(meta, "exhibition_title")
venue            = t(meta, "venue")
creator_name     = t(meta, "creator_name")
creator_qid      = t(meta, "creator_qid")

items = []
for item in root.findall("items/item"):
    dim = item.find("dimensions")
    h = t(dim, "height_cm") if dim is not None else ""
    w = t(dim, "width_cm")  if dim is not None else ""
    d = t(dim, "depth_cm")  if dim is not None else ""
    dim_str = ""
    if h and w:
        dim_str = f"{h} × {w}" + (f" × {d} cm" if d else " cm")
    items.append({
        "title":           t(item, "title"),
        "date":            t(item, "date"),
        "object_type":     t(item, "object_type"),
        "object_type_qid": t(item, "object_type_qid"),
        "materials":       t(item, "materials"),
        "dimensions":      dim_str,
        "catalog_number":  t(item, "catalog_number"),
        "inventory_number":t(item, "inventory_number"),
        "location":        t(item, "location"),
        "notes":           t(item, "notes"),
    })

print(f"Loaded {len(items)} catalogue entries")
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Creator:** [{{< meta title >}}](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q312793) (Q312793) &ensp;|&ensp;
**Venue:** [Sprengel Museum Hannover](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q510144) &ensp;|&ensp;
**Source:** OCR-processed exhibition catalogue, `catalogues/scan-2/`
:::

## Source Catalogue

```{python}
#| label: dnb-metadata
import requests
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML

DNB_IDN    = "957409494"
SRU_URL    = (f"https://services.dnb.de/sru/dnb?version=1.1"
              f"&operation=searchRetrieve"
              f"&query=idn%3D{DNB_IDN}"
              f"&recordSchema=MARC21-xml")
MARC       = "http://www.loc.gov/MARC21/slim"
CACHE_FILE = Path("catalogues/scan-2/dnb-cache.xml")

if CACHE_FILE.exists():
    xml_text = CACHE_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
else:
    resp = requests.get(SRU_URL, headers={"User-Agent": "linked-open-exhibition/1.0"}, timeout=15)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    xml_text = resp.text
    CACHE_FILE.write_text(xml_text, encoding="utf-8")

rec = ET.fromstring(xml_text).find(f".//{{{MARC}}}record")

def marc_sub(rec, tag, code):
    """First matching subfield value."""
    el = rec.find(f".//{{{MARC}}}datafield[@tag='{tag}']/{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='{code}']")
    return el.text.strip() if el is not None and el.text else ""

def marc_subs(rec, tag, code):
    """All matching subfield values."""
    return [e.text.strip() for e in
            rec.findall(f".//{{{MARC}}}datafield[@tag='{tag}']/{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='{code}']")
            if e.text]

# --- extract fields -------------------------------------------------------
title      = marc_sub(rec, "245", "a")
subtitle   = marc_sub(rec, "245", "b")
resp_stmt  = marc_sub(rec, "245", "c")   # responsibility statement
place      = marc_sub(rec, "264", "a")
publisher  = marc_sub(rec, "264", "b")
year       = marc_sub(rec, "264", "c")
extent     = marc_sub(rec, "300", "a")
ill        = marc_sub(rec, "300", "b")
dimensions = marc_sub(rec, "300", "c")
languages  = marc_subs(rec, "041", "a")
isbns      = marc_subs(rec, "020", "a")
isbn13     = next((i for i in isbns if len(i) == 13), isbns[0] if isbns else "")

# contributors: tag 700 (persons) and 710 (corporate bodies)
contributors = []
for df in rec.findall(f".//{{{MARC}}}datafield[@tag='700']"):
    name = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='a']") if e.text), "")
    role = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='e']") if e.text), "")
    gnd  = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='0']") if e.text and "d-nb.info/gnd" in e.text), "")
    if name:
        contributors.append({"name": name, "role": role, "gnd": gnd})
for df in rec.findall(f".//{{{MARC}}}datafield[@tag='710']"):
    name = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='a']") if e.text), "")
    role = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='e']") if e.text), "")
    gnd  = next((e.text for e in df.findall(f"{{{MARC}}}subfield[@code='0']") if e.text and "d-nb.info/gnd" in e.text), "")
    if name:
        contributors.append({"name": name, "role": role, "gnd": gnd})

# --- cover image (Open Library, public API, no auth) ----------------------
cover_url = f"https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/isbn/{isbn13}-L.jpg" if isbn13 else ""

# --- build HTML card -------------------------------------------------------
contrib_items = []
for c in contributors:
    link_open  = f'<a href="{c["gnd"]}" target="_blank">' if c["gnd"] else ""
    link_close = "</a>" if c["gnd"] else ""
    role_span  = f' <span class="text-muted">({c["role"]})</span>' if c["role"] else ""
    contrib_items.append(f"<li>{link_open}{c['name']}{link_close}{role_span}</li>")
contrib_html = "".join(contrib_items)
lang_map = {"ger": "German", "eng": "English"}
lang_str  = ", ".join(lang_map.get(l, l) for l in languages)
isbn_html = " &ensp;|&ensp; ".join(f'<code>{i}</code>' for i in isbns)

img_block = (
    f'<a href="https://d-nb.info/{DNB_IDN}" target="_blank">'
    f'<img src="{cover_url}" alt="Cover: {title}" '
    f'style="max-height:220px;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.25);border-radius:3px;">'
    f'</a>'
) if cover_url else ""

card = f"""
<div class="card mb-4">
  <div class="card-body">
    <div class="row g-3">
      <div class="col-auto">{img_block}</div>
      <div class="col">
        <h5 class="card-title mb-1">
          <a href="https://d-nb.info/{DNB_IDN}" target="_blank">{title}</a>
        </h5>
        <p class="text-muted mb-2" style="font-size:.9rem">{subtitle}</p>
        <table class="table table-sm table-borderless mb-2" style="font-size:.875rem">
          <tbody>
            <tr><th scope="row" style="width:140px">Responsibility</th><td>{resp_stmt}</td></tr>
            <tr><th scope="row">Publisher</th><td>{place}: {publisher}, {year}</td></tr>
            <tr><th scope="row">Extent</th><td>{extent} {ill} — {dimensions}</td></tr>
            <tr><th scope="row">Language</th><td>{lang_str}</td></tr>
            <tr><th scope="row">ISBN</th><td>{isbn_html}</td></tr>
            <tr><th scope="row">DNB record</th>
                <td><a href="https://d-nb.info/{DNB_IDN}" target="_blank">d-nb.info/{DNB_IDN}</a></td></tr>
          </tbody>
        </table>
        <p class="mb-1" style="font-size:.875rem"><strong>Contributors</strong></p>
        <ul class="mb-0" style="font-size:.875rem">{contrib_html}</ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
"""
HTML(card)
```

## Data Sources

### Input XML

The catalogue items are stored in a structured XML file validated against a custom DTD:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [`catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.xml`](catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.xml) | Exhibition catalogue data — 73 items |
| [`catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.dtd`](catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.dtd) | Document Type Definition (DTD) |

### Schema Mappings

Each element in the DTD is aligned to two Linked Data vocabularies:

| XML element | Wikidata property | CIDOC CRM |
|-------------|-------------------|-----------|
| `title` | [P1476](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P1476) title | P102_has_title |
| `creator` | [P170](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P170) creator | P14_carried_out_by |
| `date` | [P571](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P571) inception | P108_has_produced |
| `object_type` | [P31](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P31) instance of | — |
| `materials` | [P186](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P186) material used | P45_consists_of |
| `dimensions` | [P2048](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P2048)/[P2049](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P2049)/[P2610](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P2610) | P43_has_dimension |
| `inventory_number` | [P217](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P217) inventory number | P1_is_identified_by (E42) |
| `catalog_number` | [P528](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P528) catalogue code | P1_is_identified_by (E42) |
| `location` | [P276](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P276) location | — |
| `exhibited_at` | [P1431](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Property:P1431) participant | E7_Activity |

### Viewing and Editing the XML

::: {.callout-tip}
**Recommended tool — [XML Notepad](https://microsoft.github.io/XmlNotepad/)** (Microsoft, free, Windows)

XML Notepad provides a tree view of the XML document, DTD-aware validation, XPath search, and syntax highlighting. Open `catalogue-items.xml` directly; it will automatically locate `catalogue-items.dtd` in the same folder and report any validation errors.

Alternatives: [oXygen XML Editor](https://www.oxygenxml.com/) (cross-platform, commercial), [VSCodium](https://vscodium.com/) with the [XML extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=redhat.vscode-xml).
:::

## Full Catalogue Table

```{python}
#| label: full-table
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import HTML

df = pd.DataFrame(items)
df.columns = ["Title", "Date", "Type", "Type QID", "Materials",
              "Dimensions", "Cat. No.", "Inv. No.", "Location", "Notes"]
df = df.drop(columns=["Type QID"])
df.index = range(1, len(df) + 1)

# Truncate very long material strings for readability
df["Materials"] = df["Materials"].str[:60].where(
    df["Materials"].str.len() <= 60,
    df["Materials"].str[:60] + "…"
)

HTML(df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-hover", border=0, escape=False))
```

---

## Works in the Exhibition

```{python}
#| label: cards

# Group items by object type for display
from collections import Counter
from IPython.display import HTML
type_counts = Counter(i["object_type"] or "unclassified" for i in items)

def wd_badge(qid, label, colour="secondary"):
    if qid:
        return (f'<a href="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/{qid}" target="_blank" '
                f'class="badge bg-{colour} text-decoration-none me-1">{label}</a>')
    return f'<span class="badge bg-{colour} me-1">{label}</span>'

cards_html = '<div class="row row-cols-1 row-cols-md-2 row-cols-lg-3 g-3 mt-1">\n'

for item in items:
    title = item["title"] or "(untitled)"
    date  = item["date"]
    otype = item["object_type"]
    oqid  = item["object_type_qid"]
    mats  = item["materials"]
    dims  = item["dimensions"]
    cat_n = item["catalog_number"]
    inv_n = item["inventory_number"]
    notes = item["notes"]

    type_badge = wd_badge(oqid, otype, "primary") if otype else ""
    cat_badge  = (f'<span class="badge bg-light text-dark border me-1">cat. {cat_n}</span>'
                  if cat_n else "")

    meta_lines = []
    if date:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Date:</b> {date}")
    if mats:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Materials:</b> {mats}")
    if dims:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Dimensions:</b> {dims}")
    if inv_n:  meta_lines.append(f"<b>Inventory:</b> {inv_n}")
    if notes:  meta_lines.append(f"<b>Note:</b> {notes}")
    meta_html = "<br>".join(meta_lines)

    cards_html += f"""  <div class="col">
    <div class="card h-100 shadow-sm">
      <div class="card-body">
        <h6 class="card-title fw-semibold">{title}</h6>
        <p class="card-text mb-1">{type_badge}{cat_badge}</p>
        <p class="card-text small text-muted">{meta_html}</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
"""

cards_html += "</div>\n"
HTML(cards_html)
```

---

## Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: summary
from IPython.display import HTML
import pandas as pd

# Object type breakdown
type_df = (pd.DataFrame(list(type_counts.items()), columns=["Object Type", "Count"])
             .sort_values("Count", ascending=False)
             .reset_index(drop=True))
type_df.index = range(1, len(type_df) + 1)

# Date breakdown (year only)
year_counts = Counter()
for i in items:
    d = i["date"]
    if d:
        year = d[:4] if len(d) >= 4 else d
        year_counts[year] += 1

year_df = (pd.DataFrame(list(year_counts.items()), columns=["Year / Period", "Count"])
             .sort_values("Year / Period")
             .reset_index(drop=True))
year_df.index = range(1, len(year_df) + 1)

html = (
    '<div class="row">'
    '<div class="col-md-5">'
    + type_df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0, escape=False)
    + '</div><div class="col-md-4 offset-md-1">'
    + year_df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0, escape=False)
    + '</div></div>'
)
HTML(html)
```
